author: The Works of John Cassian
book: The Seven Books of John Cassian on the Incarnation of the Lord, Against Nestorius.

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: The Seven Books of John Cassian on the Incarnation of the Lord, Against Nestorius.
Chapter XI. He returns to the prophecy of Isaiah.

ccel.title: NPNF-211. Sulpitius Severus, Vincent of Lerins, John Cassian

source.title: NPNF-211. Sulpitius Severus, Vincent of Lerins, John Cassian

title: Chapter XI. He returns to the prophecy of Isaiah.

source.title: NPNF-211. Sulpitius Severus, Vincent of Lerins, John Cassian

verses.book: The Seven Books of John Cassian on the Incarnation of the Lord, Against Nestorius.
verses.text: “The labour,” says he, “of Egypt, and the merchandize of Ethiopia, and the Sabæans, men of stature shall come over to thee.”

verses.book: The Seven Books of John Cassian on the Incarnation of the Lord, Against Nestorius.
verses.text: No one can doubt that in these names of different nations is signified the coming of the nations who were to believe.

verses.book: The Seven Books of John Cassian on the Incarnation of the Lord, Against Nestorius.
verses.text: But you cannot deny that the nations have come over to Christ, for since the name of Christianity has arisen, they have come over to the Lord Jesus Christ not only in faith but actually in name.

verses.book: The Seven Books of John Cassian on the Incarnation of the Lord, Against Nestorius.
verses.text: For since they are called what they really are, that which was the work of faith becomes the token by which they are named.

verses.book: The Seven Books of John Cassian on the Incarnation of the Lord, Against Nestorius.
verses.text: “They shall,” he says, “come over to thee and shall be thine: they shall walk after thee bound with manacles.”

verses.book: The Seven Books of John Cassian on the Incarnation of the Lord, Against Nestorius.
verses.text: As there are chains of coercion, so too there are chains of love, as the Lord says: “I drew them with chains of love.”

verses.book: The Seven Books of John Cassian on the Incarnation of the Lord, Against Nestorius.
verses.text: For indeed great are these chains, and chains of ineffable love, for those who are bound with them rejoice in their fetters.

verses.book: The Seven Books of John Cassian on the Incarnation of the Lord, Against Nestorius.
verses.text: Do you want to know whether this is true?

verses.book: The Seven Books of John Cassian on the Incarnation of the Lord, Against Nestorius.
verses.text: Hear how the Apostle Paul exults and rejoices in his chains, when he says: “I therefore a prisoner in the Lord beseech you.”

verses.book: The Seven Books of John Cassian on the Incarnation of the Lord, Against Nestorius.
verses.text: And again: “I beseech thee, whereas thou art such an one as Paul the aged, and now a prisoner also of Jesus Christ.”

verses.book: The Seven Books of John Cassian on the Incarnation of the Lord, Against Nestorius.
verses.text: You see how he rejoiced in the dignity of his chains, by the example of which he actually stirred up others.

verses.book: The Seven Books of John Cassian on the Incarnation of the Lord, Against Nestorius.
verses.text: But there can be no doubt that where there is single-minded love of the Lord, there is also single-minded delight in chains worn for the Lord’s sake: as it is written: “But the multitude of the believers was of one heart and one soul.”

verses.book: The Seven Books of John Cassian on the Incarnation of the Lord, Against Nestorius.
verses.text: “And they shall worship thee,” he says, “and shall make supplication to thee: for in thee is God, and there is no God beside thee.”

verses.book: The Seven Books of John Cassian on the Incarnation of the Lord, Against Nestorius.
verses.text: The Apostle clearly explains the prophet’s words, when he says that “God was in Christ reconciling the world to Himself.”

verses.book: The Seven Books of John Cassian on the Incarnation of the Lord, Against Nestorius.
verses.text: “In Thee then,” he says, “is God and there is no God beside thee.”

verses.book: The Seven Books of John Cassian on the Incarnation of the Lord, Against Nestorius.
verses.text: When the prophet says “In Thee is God,” most admirably does he point not merely to Him who was visible, but to Him who was in what was visible, distinguishing the indweller from Him in whom He dwelt, by pointing out the two natures, not by denying the unity (of Person).
